title: CITES-Authorized Iran Caviar (Persian Sturgeon) Export Quota, 2006
source_org: CITES Secretariat (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species), as reported by UN News
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION from UN News article text via WebFetch. Direct access to cites.org's own quota-document archive (e.g. cites.org/sites/default/files/common/quotas/2006/Sturgeon2006.pdf) was attempted but blocked by a Cloudflare bot-verification CAPTCHA challenge; per project safety rules, CAPTCHA bypass was not attempted. UN News, as the official news service of the United Nations reporting directly on a CITES Secretariat announcement, is treated as a reliable secondary citation of the primary quota figure.
license_terms: UN News content, public UN information service.
notes: This is a REGULATORY CEILING (permitted export volume under international treaty), not an actual production/export figure -- distinct in kind from the sibling dataset ifo-caviar-production-export-compiled/, which reports actual reported output. Useful as a rough proxy: 44.37 tonnes authorized in 2006 is roughly 3.6x Iran's actual reported production a decade-plus later (12t in 2021) and ~2.4x the FY2022-23 production (18.5t), illustrating the scale of the wild-catch-era Caspian caviar trade relative to today's farmed output. This dataset intentionally contains only ONE row; a fuller CITES quota time series (1998-2007) was sought but not obtainable within this session without CAPTCHA bypass -- see failures.
failures: cites.org blocked all direct access attempts (both curl and Claude Browser) with a Cloudflare 'Verify you are human' CAPTCHA challenge. Per project safety rules (never bypass/complete CAPTCHAs), no further attempt was made. A multi-year CITES sturgeon quota time series for Iran (which likely exists at cites.org/sites/default/files/common/quotas/<year>/Sturgeon<year>.pdf for years 1998-2007+) could NOT be retrieved as a result. Future sessions with different tooling/access may be able to retrieve this directly from CITES.
